html:
Exclusive to:

“Tomatoes are an excellent source of vitamins, and make for a convenient projectile.”

– W

For detailed farming informations, see Farm Plant.

The Toma Roots is an object exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in the Reap What You Sow update.

The Toma Root Plant is an object exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in the Reap What You Sow update. It can be obtained by planting Spiky Seeds (also named Toma Root Seeds, if researched with the Gardeneer Hat) into Farm Soil or on the ground as Wormwood. There is a chance to grow a Toma Root Plant by planting regular Seeds.

The plant's favourite seasons are Autumn, Spring and Summer, so in Winter it will grow slower and accumulate more Stress points. It consumes a large amount of water, draining 16.8 ground moisture per day. When planted, Toma Roots will consume 1 unit of Growth Formula and 1 unit of Compost while enriching the soil with 2 units of Manure.

When harvesting in the full stage, it will give 1 Toma Root and 0-2 Spiky/Toma Root Seeds depending on the amount of stress points the plant accumulated. It can also grow into a Giant Toma Root, if the plant accumulated only 0-1 stress points. When picked and hammered, it will drop 2-3 Toma Roots and 2-3 Spiky/Toma Roo Seeds.

The Toma Root stays 4 days on the plant before rotting and the Giant Toma Root stays 6 Days on the plant. If it has been planted by Wormwood, it lasts 50% longer before spoiling. Picking the plant in its rotten stage will give 1 Rot (normal) or 3 Rot and 1 Toma Root Seed (giant). After 4 to 5 days of being in the rotten stage, a non-giant Toma Root Plant grows back, but the new plant cannot become giant.

When picking up a rotten plant, a Fruit Fly has a 5% chance to spawn.

Gallery

  • Toma Roots in Plant Registry.
DSTDSRoGSWHam

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Rễ Toma là một vật phẩm độc quyền của Don't Starve Together, được giới thiệu trong bản cập nhật Reap What You Sow.

{{for|detailed farming informations|Farm Plant}}

The '''Toma Roots''' is an object exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', introduced in the ''[[Reap What You Sow]]'' update. 

The '''Toma Root Plant''' is an object exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', introduced in the ''[[Reap What You Sow]]'' update. It can be obtained by planting [[Crop Seeds/DST#Spiky Seeds|Spiky Seeds]] (also named Toma Root Seeds, if researched with the [[Gardeneer Hat]]) into [[Farm Soil]] or on the ground as [[Wormwood]]. There is a chance to grow a Toma Root Plant by planting regular [[Seeds]].

The plant's favourite seasons are [[Autumn]], [[Spring]] and [[Summer]], so in [[Winter]] it will grow slower and accumulate more [[Farm Plant#Stress|Stress]] points. It consumes a large amount of water, draining 16.8 [[Farm Plant#Ground Moisture|ground moisture]] per day. When planted, Toma Roots will [[Farm Plant#Ground Nutrient|consume]] 1 unit of Growth Formula and 1 unit of Compost while enriching the soil with 2 units of Manure.

When harvesting in the full stage, it will give 1 [[Toma Root]] and 0-2 Spiky/Toma Root Seeds depending on the amount of stress points the plant accumulated. It can also grow into a [[Giant Crops#Giant Toma Root|Giant Toma Root]], if the plant accumulated only 0-1 stress points. When picked and [[hammer]]ed, it will drop 2-3 Toma Roots and 2-3 Spiky/Toma Roo Seeds.

The Toma Root stays 4 days on the plant before rotting and the Giant Toma Root stays 6 [[Day]]s on the plant. If it has been planted by Wormwood, it lasts 50% longer before spoiling. Picking the plant in its rotten stage will give 1 [[Rot]] (normal) or 3 Rot and 1 Toma Root Seed (giant). After 4 to 5 days of being in the rotten stage, a non-giant Toma Root Plant grows back, but the new plant cannot become giant.

When picking up a rotten plant, a [[Fruit Fly]] has a 5% chance to spawn.
